raw_input:
[4.0/5] Nice Brush | Similar To We Love Doodles & Chris Christensen | Cats Love It | Easily Removes Hair If I didn't already own & love my "We Love Doodles" brand of big slicker brush, I would have rated this brush at a full 5 stars. Mostly what I don't care for this brush vs Doodles is the handle. It is a little more narrow so it's a teensy bit less comfortable to hold. I have carpel tunnel. I've been grooming cats furever & grooming English angora rabbits for well over a decade.

🐱For short & long haired cats, this brush works great. All of my cats love this brush & my Doodles brand. I can comb all of them except for my 14yo with a surprising amount of pressure & they roll around purring. Tons of hair gets picked up into the tines. Unlike some styles of brushes & combs, no hair is left behind around the tail area.

Removing the hair from the brush is very easy. Start at one side from the bottom to loosen & then move to the other side. Keep working your way around & it comes off in a nice clean loose mat. I actually prefer these types of slicker brushes over self cleaning brushes & also over brushes with the little plastic beads on the ends. If you groom your animal properly, you don't need little balls on the ends of the tines. Those balls also make it a little harder to get the brush fully clean of hair.

Cosmetically, I like the look of the brush much better than my Doodles brush. This is all black with a more classy branding in small white text on the head. We Love Doodles is blue & loudly brands itself on the head.

🐰I don't recommend this style of brush for English angora rabbits. I have found that my bunnies seem to be more comfortable with smaller slickers with shorter tines. You'd think with their dense long coats, you'd want something large with long tines, but it really doesn't work out like you'd expect. At least for me it doesn't.

🐰Recommended for English Angora Rabbits:

🐇Doggy Man sells a small soft slicker brush that my angora rabbits love. It's plastic with a little blue ring on the handle. I have purchased/replaced many of them over the years (the handles tend to break over time) because they work well.
🌐 https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B001NLVFH2
🐇I also recommend if money allows, a small slicker brush by Chris Christensen. I've never needed to replace it.
🌐 https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B00GU7NKWW

Personally, I'd shop between this brand (Rita's), We Love Doodles, & Chris Christensen. Chris Christensen is top of the line. They are worth it if you have the money. We Love Doodles has been the middle ground for me. They were originally priced at $50, but the price has been a lot lower for a while now. Rita's is currently the second most expensive & probably the lowest quality of the three brands. However, I've been using it for the least amount of time, so it's too early to know for sure. So far I like all three.

Chris Christensen: Made in Germany $75.99
https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product//B00KAJTPZ2

We Love Doodles: Made in USA $29.99
https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B09G981NXV

Rita's: Made in China $36.99 [5.0/5] Nice brush! [[VIDEOID:a512021e054bf612bf9a5fb5fc70a2c2]] Nice brush, easy to use, easy to clean, everything I wanted and needed in a brush, and our puppy is loving it! At first a bit hesitant as it is a little rough but that didn’t last long and as you can see she is throughly enjoying it! [5.0/5] Good for shedders Now that we are in the summer time our furry friends have begun to shed. This brush is doing it's part to ensure all that loose fuzz doesnt end up on my floor or in my air vents.

Its durable plastic with metal bristles. Catches the loose fur fairly well with hurting my dog one bit. [4.0/5] Great pet brush but difficult to clean This is a great pet hair brush and both my yellow lab and my cats love it. It has much longer bristles/pins than previous brushes I've used so it can hold more hair before needing cleaning. This also is the cause of the negative for this brush though which is that it is quite hard to clean out compared to brushes that have an ejector feature that pushes the hair off the bristles. If this brush included that feature it'd be perfect. [5.0/5] Works wonders Have an Aussie and this is a must have during shedding seasons. [4.0/5] Decent slicker brush I got this brush to use on my maltipoo dog. The brush does a good job of fluffing up her hair and making her feel super soft. The brush seems like it is well made. She doesn't fight me on brushing her with this brush the way she did with another brush we had that has those little balls on the ends of the pins. I was concerned that she wouldn't like this brush because the ends are unfinished pins, but she seems to like this brush better. It also seems to go through her hair with greater ease than the other brush we had been using. [5.0/5] Extremely handy I bought these because my pup has weeping eyes, which can cause problems if not taken care of and can also stain his fur. I bought these to help with his weeping eyes and have used them consistently for a few days now. I didn’t really think a wipe would make that much of a difference, but he doesn’t look like he has weeping eyes at all unless you look closely.
Aside from weeping eyes, I have found these to be quite handy all around. My pup sometimes has trouble not getting a mess on his fur when pottying and these are clutch in those moments. It is also nice for cleaning off his paws after he has stepped in something that has gotten them dirty.
Overall, I bought these to help me with one purpose - taking care of my pup’s weepy eyes, but wound up finding them useful in a number of cases. These are definitely worth having and comes in a doubly-sealed package (the snap top and the tap that lifts up and reseals after use), which keeps them fresh. I highly recommend these and will purchase in the future. [5.0/5] GREAT for De-knotting Long Fur - Pricey We have an aging 16 year old long haired Main Coon cat, and she's starting to get clumps/knots in her fur. Our regular brush just wasn't really doing it well enough at preventing/removing the knots. So I figured I'd give one of these "lotsa fine bristles" brushes a try.

What a difference! In just about 30 minutes, across 2 separate brushing sessions, I was able to pull out a TON of old hair and clear out several knots. You really kinda have to do short strokes with this as there are SO many tines and it'll try to pull too much at once. But what a difference it makes!

Only complaint is the price - it's far and away the most expensive brush I have, for people or pets!

RECOMMENDED [4.0/5] Great Detangler! Very large, great for any big doodle or poodle that has lots of knots and tangles. Its a very effective tool for starting a dematting process if your wanting to keep the majority of the coat. The long bristles make it easy to catch onto any knots. [4.0/5] works, but not self-cleaning This has longer than average tines, so it got down into my double-coated dog's fur. The tines are a little sharp. But it is not self-cleaning, meaning there is nothing to press and collect the fur. Because the tines are extra long, I had to empty this brush less often than with some, but it still was a pain to stop and pull the fur off. I have gotten spoiled with the self-cleaning ones. [4.0/5] Long Pin Slicker Brush [[VIDEOID:3dbb4f6afc88c6e7ba97851f064ad126]] The brush is well crafted and the pins are longer than normal. It fit comfortably in my hand and easy to use . I could easily pull hair from the pins when done brushing.

Wanted to try this on my shih tzu and found that the long pins are not the best for his shorter puppy cut. and was awkward to use on that length hair. .Recommend for dogs with longer hairstyles as has a nice quality feel and pins attached securely. [5.0/5] We like it! Including a photo of my dog for reference since she's a cutie and to show her type of hair -- as an Aussiedoodle she has a longer, wavier coat than some dogs which means our pup needs to be brushed every day. We ordered this brush after our old one gave out and we do like it! I felt like it got out her tangles without being too rough on her skin, but it was a little difficult to remove the hair from the brush after we were done with it since the bristles are so close together -- HOWEVER the bristle spacing is somewhat of a positive at the same time because it means it gave our dogs coat a very fine, very through brushing. All in all, we like the brush and I think it's very pretty! [3.0/5] does get a lot of loose hair, hard to clean I got the Rita's Pet Supplies brush for my Malinois, who sheds a lot. I had been using a brush bought from the pet store, that has the plastic tines with little balls on the end. This one was supposed to get more hair and be gentle, so I tried it.
It did get a lot of hair, and my dog seemed to be fine with it. She even turned over to let me get her belly, so no complaints there. Cleaning the brush was not that easy, as it has a large height and width, so pulling the hair out of it takes several attempts. Also, the price is about twice what I paid for my other brush, which did just as good a job. So, while this is a fine brush and does its job well, it does not justify a price that high. [4.0/5] Perfect shaped dog brush The handle of this brush is perfect for brushing out a dog. And it cleans out very easily. I felt like it did a good job brushing out my aussie and got quite a bit of hair off of her. I would definitely recommend this brush! [3.0/5] I want to love this but it needs improvement. As my title says, I want to love this but it needs improvement. First of all, my brush came damaged out of the box, I was able to luckily bend the bristles back. This brush brushes great, it gets out deep mats, and does a great job on my Pomeranians thick hair. However, it is big, bulky, and very uncomfortable to hold. I found myself often holding it from the head rather than the handle because the handle hurt my hand. The handle needs a redesign, and I may even see if I can just remove it. The bristle are very long and are the kind that can stab your fingers, I used a comb to clean them out. [5.0/5] Great for my double doodle! [[VIDEOID:49f61449a2ae453ec990179babbbc0a0]] This is the only brush that works on my Bernedoodle/Goldendoodle! He loves it as you can see if the video. Gets the rats and mats out efficiently. Strong enough for the knots but gentle enough for his sensitive skin. A win for all of us! [4.0/5] Large ,Pricey but works really well. [[VIDEOID:efb67b6214cb220065ddee3a814cbb21]] I am always dealing with shedding year round especially our longer haired cat.

This brush is larger and gets the job done fade making everyone’s coat look soft and beautiful and best of all with its long bristles, it does a great job of pulling out any loose hair.

This is perfect for fluffy, longer haired pets.

It is a bit more expensive and that is why o gave it four stars but it may be worth it just to get all that loose hair off all out four legged friends.

The bristles are not too stiff and the pets enjoy their brushing with this new Rita’s slicker brush.

Very happy with our new brush especially because of the larger size and long very fine bristles, works really well. [5.0/5] True CC Big K Knockoff I am a professional groomer and this brush is amazing! It looks, feels and functions just like the CC brush line. Just like with CC, the handle is a little small for my hand but I adjusted. This brush has cut my brush time almost in half!

response:
Brush work good on cats, dogs, long fur animals. Most pets like it, roll around, enjoy brushing. Long tines reach deep into thick coats, grab lots of loose hair. Good for mats, knots, tangles. Works on double coats, doodles, aussies, pomeranians, maine coons.

Handle uncomfortable for some, especially those with hand problems. Not self-cleaning, must pull hair out by hand. Long tines make cleaning harder. Some say cleaning take many attempts. One brush arrived damaged out of box.

Price high. Made in China. Compare to Chris Christensen (Germany, $76) and We Love Doodles (USA, $30). Rita brush cost $37. Some say not worth price difference over cheaper brushes.

Not good for short puppy cuts or english angora rabbits. Those animals do better with smaller brush, shorter tines.

One review not about brush at all. About pet eye wipes. Wipes help with weepy dog eyes, dirty paws, fur cleanup. Come in double-sealed package, stay fresh.

Professional groomer say brush similar to Chris Christensen Big K. Cut brush time in half.
